page: Amazon Web Services c6i.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server instance powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8375C processors. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 128 vCPUs derived from 64 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system operates at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz and includes a cache hierarchy of 48 KB L1d, 1 MB L2, and 54 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core.

In benchmark evaluations, the c6i.metal demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core CPU testing and Passmark CPU scores. Single-core performance falls within the average range, as shown by stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core metrics. The hardware excels in specialized vector and cryptographic operations, utilizing AVX-512 and VAES instruction sets to achieve top-tier results in ray tracing, image processing, and Passmark extended instruction tests. Memory bandwidth is strong, registering 337.58 GB/sec for large-block read operations.

This bare-metal instance does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware, meaning all storage requirements must be handled via network-attached options. Network connectivity is supported by a 50 Gbps baseline bandwidth. Given the high physical core count and compute-to-memory ratio, the instance is highly cost-efficient for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. It is well-suited for high-performance computing, batch processing, intensive web serving, software compilation, and multi-threaded database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services c6i.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server instance featuring 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz. It is configured with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing a compute-focused ratio of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage and a 50 Gbps baseline network interface. In benchmark testing, the c6i.metal delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU tests, alongside strong memory bandwidth and database processing capabilities. This bare-metal configuration is highly efficient for parallel processing tasks, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving. It is best suited for compute-intensive workloads, multi-threaded applications, and batch processing environments that require direct access to physical hardware resources without hypervisor overhead.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6i.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized instance with 128 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 50 Gbps network interface, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for intensive parallel workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6i.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized instance featuring 128 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR4 RAM, and 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A bare-metal, compute-optimized instance featuring 128 vCPUs and dedicated Intel Xeon processors for high-throughput, multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Bare-metal architecture with 128 vCPUs and 64 physical Intel Xeon cores
256 GB of DDR4 memory at 3 GHz with 2.0 GB per core density
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and AVX-512 instruction support
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput networking
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the base hardware
Best suited for compute-intensive batch processing, compilation, and web serving
